manifest.name: GraphFrontier

name: GraphFrontier

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## GraphFrontier 0.7.1

This release improves performance and interaction responsiveness on large graphs, especially around heavy graph sessions and repeated interaction workflows.

### Improvements
- Improved large-graph performance and reduced unnecessary interaction overhead.
- Restored linked-node highlighting when selecting nodes from search.
- Fixed static HTML export so exported graphs are saved as working `.html` files.
- Static HTML exports now preserve pan, zoom, hover labels, node selection, and file-content previews.
- Updated release packaging so the install zip is produced from `dist/release`.

### Install
Download `graphfrontier-0.7.1.zip` and extract it into:

`<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/graphfrontier/`

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## GraphFrontier 0.7.0

This release brings a major UX polish pass focused on layouts, selection workflows, and export.

### Highlights
- Layout workflow reworked:
  - Layout picker row uses search-style UI
  - `Save` can create a new layout when a new name is entered
  - Active layout loads automatically on graph open
  - Layout names shown without `.json`
  - Layout delete via dropdown `x` now works reliably
- Side menu improvements:
  - Submenu sections use compact transparent arrow controls
  - Open/closed submenu state is remembered globally (independent from layout)
  - Blacklist and Whitelist kept together as one block
- Selection and context actions:
  - Multi-select + box-select improvements
  - Context menu actions for linked nodes (select/pin/unpin/copy linked names/copy linked paths)
  - Linked copy output now supports line-break formatting
- Attachments behavior updates:
  - Orbit/no-physics mode improvements
  - Interaction constraints for attachments refined
- Static export expanded:
  - New export flow with custom folder+filename modal
  - First export path starts empty; later exports reuse last path
  - Exported static graph supports pan/zoom/hover and side content panel
- Stability fix:
  - Better startup timing for applying saved node positions

### Quality
- Passed `prettier --check`, `eslint`, and production build before release.

### Assets
- `graphfrontier-0.7.0.zip`
- `main.js`
- `manifest.json`
- `styles.css`
- `versions.json`

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release sharpens graph workflow speed and control, especially for dense
  maps and multi-node operations.

  ### Highlights

  - New linked-node actions in the node right-click menu:
      - Select linked nodes
      - Copy linked names
      - Copy linked paths
  - Copy output format improved for fast reuse:
      - one item per line
      - comma-separated style with no trailing comma on the last line
  - Multi-selection controls reorganized:
      - moved into a dedicated Selection collapsible section above Display
  - Selection controls restyled for consistency with side-panel controls (Hide-
    style buttons)
  - Context menu organization improved with clearer grouping/separators around
    linked copy actions

  ### UX and behavior improvements

  - Faster bulk workflows from the graph canvas:
      - select linked clusters in one click
      - export linked names/paths instantly for notes, tasks, or external tools
  - Cleaner side panel layout with clearer separation of selection settings from
    display/physics settings

  ### Quality and release hygiene

  - Formatting/lint/build pipeline verified clean
  - Version metadata synchronized across release files
  - New release artifact packaged for distribution
  
  **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/pikiby/GraphFrontier/compare/0.6.8...0.6.10

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release improves side panel usability and performance for larger graphs.

  ### What’s new

  - Added collapsible side panel sections with small, transparent arrow+text
    headers.
  - Combined Blacklist and Whitelist into one shared side panel block.
  - Improved side panel visual consistency and reduced UI noise.
  - Added visibility caching with lazy recompute for filtering logic to reduce
    repeated heavy recalculations in render/physics/interaction paths.

  ### Performance

  - Filter visibility now reuses cached results when graph/rules/search state has
    not changed.
  - Rule parsing and visibility set building are no longer recomputed on every hot-
    path call.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/pikiby/GraphFrontier/compare/0.6.6...0.6.7

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
• ## GraphFrontier 0.6.4

  This patch release improves query filtering and stabilizes
  release metadata.

  ### What’s new

  - Added name: support to Groups dropdown suggestions.
  - Added name: support to Whitelist dropdown suggestions.
  - Added name: support to Blacklist dropdown suggestions.
  - Implemented actual name: matching by node label for group
    coloring and whitelist/blacklist rule evaluation.

  ### Improvements

  - Synced plugin version metadata across release files
    (manifest, versions, static assets, dist).
  - Updated docs for supported group query patterns.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## GraphFrontier 0.6.2

### Major improvements
- Unified dropdown engine for both Search and Groups.
- Filter now works as a true physical filter across all search modes.
- Filtered-out nodes are excluded from force simulation and rendering.
- Search UI updated: compact Find/Filter toggle and inline clear button inside the input.

### Search and filtering
- Shared query workflow for Search and Groups: same source/value suggestion behavior.
- Default text input (without prefix) works as content search.
- Name mode keeps node-focused behavior; other modes support multi-match filtering.

### Physics behavior
- Active filter set now drives physics calculations.
- Non-filtered nodes are frozen while filter is active.
- Link and repel calculations are limited to the active filtered subgraph.

### UI polish
- Find/Filter toggle size aligned with side toggles (Show grid style).
- Inline clear icon appears only when search field is not empty.
- Cleaner filtered rendering for better readability.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
GraphFrontier v0.6.0
This release marks a major stability and usability step for GraphFrontier as an independent graph engine for Obsidian.

Highlights
New physics improvements for large graphs with much smoother movement
New Repel radius control for distance-limited repulsion
Better handling of orphans and attachments in mixed scenarios
New attachment-only node behavior for stable, non-jittery motion
Improved balancing between main nodes, orphan nodes, and attachment-related nodes
Orbit-pinned regular nodes now participate in repulsion logic more naturally
Updated command/hotkey naming (cleaner labels, no redundant suffixes)
UI/UX
Continued polish of side panel controls and behavior
Consistent toggle/slider interactions
Better overall responsiveness in dense graphs
Documentation
Updated [README.md](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/home/boris/.vscode/extensions/openai.chatgpt-0.4.76-linux-x64/webview/#) with clearer product positioning and installation flow
Added support/feedback section:
Buy Me a Coffee
Discussions for ideas
Issues for bug reports
Updated [physics.md](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/home/boris/.vscode/extensions/openai.chatgpt-0.4.76-linux-x64/webview/#) with current simulation behavior
Updated [settings.md](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/home/boris/.vscode/extensions/openai.chatgpt-0.4.76-linux-x64/webview/#) with new settings coverage (Repel radius)
Demo Updates
README now includes additional demo cards:

Hotkey support
Command support
Installation
Download and extract:

[graphfrontier-0.6.0.zip](https://github.com/pikiby/GraphFrontier/releases/download/v0.6.0/graphfrontier-0.6.0.zip)
into:

<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/graphfrontier/
then enable GraphFrontier in Obsidian Community Plugins.

Feedback
Ideas and critique:

https://github.com/pikiby/GraphFrontier/discussions
Bug reports:

https://github.com/pikiby/GraphFrontier/issues
